body:
In main there are a couple of bugs that disallow running a `SplitExplicitMomentumEquation` with distributed architectures. This is because Distributed should extend the halos to avoid passing information at each substep.
However, the extension was not wired correctly and we ended up not computing sea ice rheology fields (viscosity, ice strength, etc...) on the extended halos.

This PR fixes it by adding a `materialize_solver` that is supposed to materialize the dynamics solver properties. Also a small test to check that construction occurs as intended
title: Bugfix the distributed sea ice dynamics
